introSection: Super Manège (French for Super Ride) was a steel roller coaster located at La Ronde in Montréal, Québec, Canada. Opened in 1981 as Corkscrew, it was the first inverting roller coaster at the park. The ride was built by Dutch company Vekoma. It was located between Manitou and the Monstre wooden roller coaster, and was close to Boomerang. The popular Splash ride's entrance was across the pathway from the entrance of Super Manege.

sections.title: History
sections.content: On 5 August 2019, La Ronde announced that Super Manege would close at the end of the month. It was closed down on 25 August 2019, a few hours earlier than expected following an emergency stop and evacuation. This was due to a restraint that failed on the ride. The ride was demolished afterward and was due to be replaced with a new attraction: Vipère, an Intamin ZacSpin coaster formerly located at Six Flags Magic Mountain as Green Lantern: First Flight in California. These plans were cancelled in early 2022.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Orange track and dark blue supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains were built by Arrow Dynamics. A transfer track also existed to hold a train when it is not needed.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The ride started by climbing to a height of. After a small curved drop, riders were taken down a steep drop and over a smaller hill. Riders were then sent through the double corkscrew element. Then, the ride climbed again, and turned to the right. After the drop, the 'bayerncurve' element followed, which was essentially a helix that travels up and down. The ride then followed its perimeter round and back to the station.

sections.content: * Super Manège on the parks website (French)

fullText:
Super Manège (French for Super Ride) was a steel roller coaster located at La Ronde in Montréal, Québec, Canada. Opened in 1981 as Corkscrew, it was the first inverting roller coaster at the park. The ride was built by Dutch company Vekoma. It was located between Manitou and the Monstre wooden roller coaster, and was close to Boomerang. The popular Splash ride's entrance was across the pathway from the entrance of Super Manege.

On 5 August 2019, La Ronde announced that Super Manege would close at the end of the month. It was closed down on 25 August 2019, a few hours earlier than expected following an emergency stop and evacuation. This was due to a restraint that failed on the ride. The ride was demolished afterward and was due to be replaced with a new attraction: Vipère, an Intamin ZacSpin coaster formerly located at Six Flags Magic Mountain as Green Lantern: First Flight in California. These plans were cancelled in early 2022.



Orange track and dark blue supports.

The trains were built by Arrow Dynamics. A transfer track also existed to hold a train when it is not needed.

The ride started by climbing to a height of. After a small curved drop, riders were taken down a steep drop and over a smaller hill. Riders were then sent through the double corkscrew element. Then, the ride climbed again, and turned to the right. After the drop, the 'bayerncurve' element followed, which was essentially a helix that travels up and down. The ride then followed its perimeter round and back to the station.


 * Super Manège on the parks website (French)
